arts.ttl: Moyes: I hope we can bring the old Everton back
arts.sum: The first thing I have got to try to put right is that we get results to make sure we’re going into our new stadium as a Premier League team. Everton are just one point above the relegation zone, after a run of five winless league matches, and Moyes's first priority is to retain their top-flight status as the club prepare to move into their new stadium this summer.

arts.ttl: Everton vs Aston Villa team news LIVE! New Moyes era gets underway
arts.sum: David Moyes admits he has rejoined a "different" Everton to the one he left 12 years ago, but believes new owners The Friedkin Group have already put the club on the path towards a brighter future. For many years it has been stifled because of the financial situation, the new owners are clearing as much of that up and there is still a bit to be done but the new owners have an ambition to try to get Everton back nearer the top.

arts.ttl: Ex-Villa boss Ward named Republic of Ireland head coach
arts.sum: Former Aston Villa manager Carla Ward is appointed Republic of Ireland head coach, replacing Eileen Gleeson.

arts.ttl: Yasin Ozcan plan revealed as technical director lifts lid on Aston Villa transfer
arts.sum: However, while the first – Donyell Malen – will come in and hopefully contribute quickly, the teenage centre-back is expected to remain with his current club for the rest of the season. I think he has a lot of potential.

arts.ttl: Insistence Monchi has held talks for Aston Villa transfer – But he can’t use player as makeweight
arts.sum: Monchi has been in Seville to negotiate a deal for Sevilla’s Loïc Badé but can’t use Diego Carlos as a makeweight in the deal. That’s according to Estadio Deportivo, who report the Brazilian won’t be returning to his former club in any capacity.
